news.title: Prime minister to lay foundation stone for Jaipur metro phase-II on July 4
news.para: Prime Minister Narendra Modi is inaugurating Phase II of the Jaipur Metro, a five-year project set to revolutionize connectivity along Tonk Road. This expansion includes a significant elevated stretch from Sitapura to Pinjrapole and an underground section near the airport.  Traffic management plans are in place to minimize disruption, with a focus on creating a 'Model Corridor' to ensure smooth commutes during construction.

news.title: No misappropriation of flats, TDR in Kothrud SRA scheme: Maharashtra minister
news.para: State minister Shambhuraj Desai has denied any misappropriation of flats or fraudulent transfer of development rights in Pune's Kothrud slum rehabilitation project. Responding to allegations of fake beneficiaries and financial loss, Desai stated due process was followed. The urban development department will review submitted documents and hold a meeting to address the concerns raised by MLA Sunil Kamble and other elected members regarding SRA schemes.

news.title: Aadhaar e-KYC, QR code a must for property registration in Haryana
news.para: Haryana has revolutionized property registration with a new tech-driven system. Aadhaar e-KYC, biometrics, and digital signatures are now mandatory, enhancing security and transparency. Registered documents will feature QR codes for instant verification, combating fraud. The process is streamlined, eliminating physical NOCs and allowing online tracking, document uploads, and even Tatkal upgrades for urgent needs. This initiative aims to save time and money for citizens while curbing fraudulent practices.

news.title: NCDRC orders Pune builder to refund ₹7 crore for project on government land
news.para: A real estate firm and its owner must refund over Rs7 crore to 113 homebuyers after a 14-year legal battle. The National Consumer Disputes Redressal Commission found the developer sold properties on land acquired by the government in 1978 for a public project.

news.title: Odisha RERA revises QPR format for real estate projects
news.para: Odisha Real Estate Regulatory Authority (ORERA) has revised its quarterly progress report format for real estate projects, specifically rectifying errors in the financial status pro forma. The updated format, effective from June 2026, demands clearer details on fund collection, transfers to the RERA account, and expenditures from various sources. This aims to enhance transparency and simplify tracking of project finances for homebuyers and the regulator.

news.title: Landowners equally liable with builders for incomplete housing projects: Kerala-RERA
news.para: Landowners in Kerala are now equally liable with builders for incomplete housing projects if they are financially involved. The Kerala Real Estate Regulatory Authority (K-RERA) ruled that landowners actively participating in finances or profit-sharing will be treated as 'joint promoters'. This landmark decision follows a case where a landowner mortgaged project land for a loan, defaulting and impacting homebuyers.

news.title: RBI imposes ₹66.7 lakh penalty on Bank of Baroda, GIC Housing Finance
news.para: The Reserve Bank of India has levied significant penalties on Bank of Baroda and GIC Housing Finance for regulatory breaches. Bank of Baroda faces a Rs 63.6 lakh fine for charging excess interest and delayed KYC record uploads. GIC Housing Finance has been fined Rs 3.1 lakh for failing to conduct timely risk reviews of accounts. These actions highlight the RBI's commitment to enforcing fair lending and customer identification practices.

news.title: Sobha records sales value of ₹3,656 crore in Q1 FY27
news.para: Sobha achieved sales value of ₹3,656 crore in Q1 FY27, up 76% year-on-year, with 2.34 million sq ft of area sold across 1,432 homes and plots. The company launched 6.89 million sq ft of saleable area across three projects in Bengaluru and Gurugram during the quarter.

news.title: Sunset Spaces to invest ₹325 crore in Mumbai redevelopment project
news.para: Sunset Spaces, a subsidiary of Marathon Nextgen Realty, has executed a development agreement for redevelopment of a residential society in Versova, Mumbai. The project has estimated revenue potential of over ₹450 crore.

news.title: Over 1.28 lakh pending land survey cases in Maharashtra
news.para: Maharashtra faces a backlog of over 1.28 lakh land survey cases, with resolutions averaging 121 days. The state is set to appoint licensed surveyors by March 2026, establishing clear criteria for their selection and operation. Plans are also in motion to adopt a system similar to other states, where property is officially measured before land sale deeds are registered, ensuring accurate records and streamlining the process.

news.title: Protected Aravali land cleared of illegal walls, buildings in Gurugram
news.para: Authorities in Gurugram have reclaimed nearly three acres of protected Aravali land in Behrampur village, demolishing 11 illegal structures. This significant anti-encroachment drive targeted under-construction buildings and boundary walls on land vital for groundwater recharge and wildlife corridors. The action follows years of efforts to protect this ecologically sensitive region from unauthorized development and fragmentation.

news.title: Telangana HC seeks HYDRAA, HMDA stand on three-year delay in acting against lake encroachments
news.para: The Telangana High Court has demanded explanations from HYDRAA and HMDA regarding their inaction against encroachments around Errakunta lake. An advocate's petition highlighted repeated complaints over three years, alleging selective enforcement and failure to protect the water body despite its notification. The court seeks details on steps taken to safeguard the lake and prevent illegal constructions, with the next hearing scheduled for July 16.

news.title: Max Estates records ₹1,100 crore pre-sales in Q1 FY27
news.para: Max Estates sold 487 units across its projects in Noida and Gurugram during Q1 FY27, compared to 43 units in Q1 FY26. The company achieved collections of about ₹500 crore during the quarter.

news.title: Lucknow development body nod to affordable housing plan in Anant Nagar
news.para: Lucknow Development Authority has greenlit Aditi Apartments, a significant affordable housing initiative featuring 1,392 flats in Anant Nagar. This project, designed for various income brackets, will offer 412 one-bedroom and 980 two-bedroom units.  The complex will boast five residential towers, ample parking, a substantial park, and modern amenities like rainwater harvesting and high-speed lifts, enhancing urban living.

news.title: Raymond Realty records ₹700 crore pre-sales in Q1 FY27
news.para: Raymond Realty’s pre-sales rose 129% year-on-year to ₹700 crore in Q1 FY27, despite no new residential project launch during the quarter. Collections increased 47% year-on-year to ₹550 crore.

news.title: Domestic investors account for 57% of real estate investments in H1 2026: Report
news.para: Institutional investments in Indian real estate rose 50% year-on-year to $4.5 billion in H1 2026, according to Colliers India. Domestic investor inflows stood at $2.6 billion during the period, accounting for 57% of total investments.

news.title: ACE, Japan’s Kato Works to jointly manufacture cranes in India
news.para: Action Construction Equipment and Kato Works have formed a 50:50 joint venture, ACE Kato, to manufacture high-capacity cranes in India. The JV plans to invest about ₹200 crore in an 11-acre manufacturing facility in Haryana.

news.title: Uttar Pradesh CM launches tech-driven drive for land survey, demarcation
news.para: Uttar Pradesh Chief Minister Yogi Adityanath launched the Digi Rover Special Land Survey Campaign in Saharanpur, aiming to resolve over 79,000 pending land demarcation cases statewide by August 15. Utilizing advanced GNSS technology, the initiative promises more accurate, transparent, and reliable land surveys, significantly reducing disputes and litigation. This tech-driven approach replaces traditional methods, ensuring citizens access swift and efficient land-related services, bolstering good governance and protecting farmers' rights.
